title: dovyalis longispina
intro: Dovyalis longispina, also known as the Natal plum or stinkberry, is a small tree or shrub native to southern Africa, characterized by its thorny branches and red or purple fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Dovyalis longispina include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and provide antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, infusions, and tinctures, and its fruits can also be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
